title: Wire ScenePackBuilder into AgentBackgroundService chat pipeline
description:
Integrate `ScenePackBuilder` into the main agent loop so every LLM chat interpretation receives a compact scene context.

Implementation:
1. Register `ScenePackBuilder` in DI (Program.cs).
2. In `AgentBackgroundService.HandleChatEventAsync`:
   - Build a `ScenePack` from current `WorldState` + recent events before calling the chat interpreter.
   - Pass relevant pack fields to `BuildSystemPrompt` (entity summary, task highlights).
3. Update `LlmChatInterpreter.BuildSystemPrompt` signature to accept a `ScenePack?` parameter instead of individual fields.
4. Adjust the prompt text to include scene context in a structured way (not a raw dump):
   ```
   Scene: at (-232,65,151) in survival. Nearby: 2 cows, 1 zombie (5 blocks).
   Goal: Gather 10 oak_log (3/10 collected).
   Last actions: mined oak_log +1, moved to (-230,65,150).
   ```
5. Keep the prompt compact — no more than 3-4 additional lines.

Acceptance:
- LLM prompt includes scene summary when available.
- Backward compatible: no scene pack → prompt looks as it did before.
- Tests pass.

Refs: `memorysmith_situational_awareness_design_doc_20260625T020914Z.md` Phase 1
